Patch Set 1:

as said in JIRA, deployment still fails

Patch Set 1:

this is wierd. helm deploy resulted in 30285 for the aai-graphgraph component but the values.yaml points to 303 not 302.

Patch Set 1:

templates/service.yaml:    nodePort: {{ .Values.global.nodePortPrefix | default .Values.nodePortPrefix }}{{ .Values.service.nodePort }}
values.yaml:  nodePortPrefix: 303

kubectl edit service
  - name: aai-graphgraph-8453
    nodePort: 30285

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> templates/service.yaml:    nodePort: {{ .Values.global.nodePortPrefix | default .Values.nodePortPrefix }}{{ .Values.service.nodePort }}
> values.yaml:  nodePortPrefix: 303
> 
> kubectl edit service
>   - name: aai-graphgraph-8453
>     nodePort: 30285

change has been done in components/aai-graphgraph/values.yaml:  nodePortPrefix: 303
but there is still 302 in components/aai-graphadmin/values.yaml:  nodePortPrefix: 302
I suspect this one is used...

Patch Set 1:

does graphadmin even have a nodePort anymore ?

Patch Set 1:

When I read
nodePort: {{ .Values.global.nodePortPrefix | default .Values.nodePortPrefix }}{{ .Values.service.nodePort }}
my assumption is that we try first global.nodePortPrefix

According to https://helm.sh/docs/chart_template_guide/ the dot before Values means "start at the top namespace", global.nodePortPrefix is declared at the root of aai chart and is set to 302.

as it exists we use this one...no?

Patch Set 1:

is the simplist fix to change the 85 to something in 302XX that would not conflict ?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> is the simplist fix to change the 85 to something in 302XX that would not conflict ?

yes it is an option
I supposed removing the leading . could also be OK. As far as I understand it will remove the heritage (but I need to test it - not very familiar with helm chart templating)
